---
title: "Home"
---

::: {.hero-layout}

::: {.hero-text}

# Jiregna Olani Kedida

## Biostatistics | Clinical Epidemiology | Public Health | Artificial Intelligence

PhD Candidate in Biostatistics, Epidemiology & Public Health at the University of Padova and Lecturer in Statistics at Ambo University, Ethiopia.

My research focuses on Bayesian modeling, epidemiology, machine learning, federated learning, and public health AI.

[About Me](about.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
[Research](research.qmd){.btn .btn-secondary}
[Publications](publications.qmd){.btn .btn-secondary}
[Download CV](files/Jiregna_Olani_Kedida_CV.pdf){.btn .btn-success}

:::

::: {.hero-image}

![](images/profile.jpg){.profile-image}

:::

:::

---

# Biostatistics, Epidemiology & Public Health AI Lab

A future interdisciplinary research and training laboratory focused on:
- Biostatistics
- Clinical Epidemiology
- Public Health Analytics
- Artificial Intelligence for Healthcare
- Reproducible Research

---

# Research Focus

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-4 .card-box}
## Biostatistics

Bayesian modeling, multilevel models, survival analysis, clinical prediction modeling, and statistical inference.
:::

::: {.g-col-4 .card-box}
## Epidemiology

Maternal and child health, disease burden estimation, clinical studies, and public health analytics.
:::

::: {.g-col-4 .card-box}
## Public Health AI

Machine learning, federated learning, interpretable AI, and privacy-preserving healthcare analytics.
:::

:::

---

# Current Academic Direction

Current research interests include:
- Bayesian spatiotemporal malaria modeling
- Maternal and newborn health analytics
- Federated learning for healthcare
- Public health AI
- Reproducible statistical research
